item.text: I’ll trace the checkout discount path and its tests/configuration, then report the exact failure condition and evidence. I’ll keep the workspace unchanged.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/checkout.js; sed -n '1,260p' src/discount.js; sed -n '1,300p' test/checkout.test.js; sed -n '1,240p' README.md; sed -n '1,160p' package.json"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/checkout.js; sed -n '1,260p' src/discount.js; sed -n '1,300p' test/checkout.test.js; sed -n '1,240p' README.md; sed -n '1,160p' package.json"

item.text: The boundary condition is already suspect: the product rule and test say “$100.00 or more,” while the implementation uses a strict greater-than check. I’m verifying the observed failure and checking whether anything else contributes.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc 'npm test; git status --short; git log --oneline --decorate -8 --all; git blame -L 1,40 -- src/discount.js'
/bin/zsh -lc 'npm test; git status --short; git log --oneline --decorate -8 --all; git blame -L 1,40 -- src/discount.js'

item.text:
Root cause: an off-by-one boundary error in [src/discount.js](/var/folders/tt/fpnrgdcj5zgfw5ytq9qsq7f80000gn/T/diagnose-none-work-nlYikF/src/discount.js:10).

The requirement is “$100.00 or more,” but the implementation checks:

```js
subtotalCents > GOLD_MINIMUM_CENTS
```

Consequently:

- Exactly `10_000` cents receives no discount.
- `10_001` cents and above receives 10%.
- Below `10_000` correctly receives none.

This explains why the issue occurs only sometimes: eligible Gold customers are missed specifically when their subtotal is exactly $100.00. The test at [test/checkout.test.js:16](/var/folders/tt/fpnrgdcj5zgfw5ytq9qsq7f80000gn/T/diagnose-none-work-nlYikF/test/checkout.test.js:16) reproduces it, failing with expected `1000`, actual `0`; the other four tests pass.

The condition should logically be `>=`, though I made no file changes as requested.
